title: Always connect to the stdout and stderr of stream
body:
If the stdout and stderr are not attach, podman will at least get
a messsage that the container has completed and finish.

This fixes the
`podman run -a stdin fedora true`

Hang issue.
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: @rhatdan I'm not sure I would consider #1229 a bug. That is the way the flags are intended to work, per Docker.
I think this also breaks `podman run --attach stdout fedora ls /doesnotexist` - should print nothing

I believe Docker behaviour is broken.  I think this is the correct behaviour.  Having a process stick around and a user no really sure how to stop it, does not make sense.

This still gives no output.
```
# ./bin/podman run --attach stdout fedora ls /doesnotexist
# 
```

This change only effects the change where the user has only specified `--attach stdin`, I think the application exiting if he also attached either stderr or stdout, is why this app is broken.

It should not change behaviour depending on what is attached, when the container exits the command should exit.

> podman run --attach stdout fedora ls /doesnotexist

I've tried the patch and I don't get any output from the stderr
Anyone got a build (f27/28) I could play with?

Hm. We do have guards against copying to nonexistant streams in `redirectResponseToOutputStreams()` - so all this patch really does is cause us to drain the streams from the container, but not necessarily to write them to the terminal...

Alright, I think this is probably fine.
Can I take that as two LGTM?

Eh, sure. Let's merge this so we get a build in the COPR that @edsantiago can test. We can revert the change if it breaks tests.
@rh-atomic-bot r+

:pushpin: Commit 715ab9a has been approved by `mheon`
